title: To His Book (3)
content:
Be bold, my Book, nor be abash'd, or fear
     The cutting thumb-nail, or the brow severe;
     But by the Muses swear, all here is good,
     If but well read, or ill read, understood.
meaning:
Robert Herrick's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "To His Book (3)"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
